args.query: SELECT "Index", SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as up_days, SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as down_days FROM index_trade WHERE "Index" IN ('NYA', 'IXIC', 'GSPTSE') AND "Date" LIKE '%2018%' GROUP BY "Index"

exec_args.sql: SELECT "Index", SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as up_days, SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as down_days FROM index_trade WHERE "Index" IN ('NYA', 'IXIC', 'GSPTSE') AND "Date" LIKE '%2018%' GROUP BY "Index"

result.preview: "Among the North American stock indices, only the NASDAQ (IXIC) had more up days (131) than down days (120) in 2018. The other North American indices, NYSE Composite (NYA) and S&P/TSX Composite (GSPTSE), had more down days than up days."

args.answer: Among the North American stock indices, only the NASDAQ (IXIC) had more up days (131) than down days (120) in 2018. The other North American indices, NYSE Composite (NYA) and S&P/TSX Composite (GSPTSE), had more down days than up days.

val_args.answer: Among the North American stock indices, only the NASDAQ (IXIC) had more up days (131) than down days (120) in 2018. The other North American indices, NYSE Composite (NYA) and S&P/TSX Composite (GSPTSE), had more down days than up days.
